title: Immersion
definition: Pupils are immersed and genuinely engaged in the cultural or artistic encounter, practice, or knowledge being explored. They show attentiveness and curiosity.

title: Vocabulary
definition: Pupils acquire new terms related to the cultural and artistic fields addressed.

title: Discoveries
definition: Pupils discover characters, works, places, events, and/or artistic or cultural movements through encounters.

title: Multisensoriality
definition: Pupils draw on a range of senses to appreciate and question artistic works, objects, or cultural sites.

title: Expressions
definition: Pupils use, and sometimes combine, artistic and/or cultural tools and media to express sensations, emotions, and ideas.

title: Experimentation
definition: Pupils explore techniques, tools, and materials. They take initiatives and reasonable risks within a learning-by-doing approach.

title: Inquiry
definition: Pupils question the origins of the work and/or artistic practice, its place in history (past and present), and its relationship to traditions (local, cultural, etc.).

title: Distinction
definition: Pupils use one or several criteria (sensory/emotional, technical, historical, cultural, etc.) to differentiate cultural and artistic practices or creations.

title: Mutual listening
definition: Pupils are attentive and receptive to one another’s experiences.

title: Collective creation
definition: Pupils organise themselves by sharing roles and coordinating tasks within a group to produce a collective work.

title: Memories (traces)
definition: Pupils keep meaningful records of their cultural and artistic experiences.

title: Meaning-making
definition: Pupils connect past and present cultural and artistic experiences to build meaning (for instance through a narrative or explanation).

title: Sharing
definition: Pupils share the processes and/or artistic creations they have contributed to with other members of the educational community.

title: Transfer
definition: Pupils identify and transfer learning from the experience to other school subjects and/or transversal skills.

title: Pathway
definition: I design my intervention in connection with pupils’ PECA pathway, ensuring diversity and progression in learning.

title: Encounter – Knowledge – Practice
definition: I combine at least two of the three PECA components with a view to complementarity.

title: Objectives
definition: I define the objectives of the intervention beforehand and present them to pupils. In partnerships, objectives are set jointly.

title: Space
definition: I look for a suitable space for the intervention, whether in school and/or in a cultural or artistic venue.

title: Materials
definition: I provide functional, good-quality materials for pupils.

title: Budget
definition: Together with the school management, I set a budget ensuring all pupils can participate and seek possible funding from FW-B, etc.

title: Duration
definition: I plan an appropriate duration for pupil engagement, both for the intervention as a whole and for individual sessions.

title: Process
definition: I value the process as much as the final creation (performance, exhibition, etc.).

title: Respect & trust
definition: I show respect and trust towards pupils and partners and value everyone’s contribution.

title: Self-assessment
definition: I plan moments and tools for pupils to step back, including records that foster discussion and critical thinking.

title: Reflexivity
definition: I evaluate the intervention in relation to the objectives set.

title: Levels of difficulty
definition: I design tasks with an appropriate difficulty level so each pupil can thrive.

title: Interdisciplinarity
definition: I involve or connect the work with other school subjects.

title: Pupils' context
definition: I create links with pupils’ (potential) interests, current events, and/or social-cultural backgrounds.

title: Local resources
definition: I engage local people or resources (cultural organisations, non-profits, etc.) to set up activities inside or outside the school (visits to cultural/artistic venues, heritage sites, etc.).

title: Family involvement
definition: I plan moments and tools to involve families during the process and/or at the end of the intervention.

title: Collaboration
definition: In partnerships, partners consider the specificities of each context (classroom, artistic, cultural). They agree on the ‘conditions’ cells and define roles and responsibilities.

title: Project-based approach
definition: I embed the intervention within a project-based pedagogy (or similar).

title: Communication
definition: I explain the intervention, its stages, and/or expected outcomes to school management, colleagues, families, and/or colleagues in other schools.

title: Representations
definition: I question pupils on their representations of cultural and/or artistic practice, the artist, the work, and/or heritage.

title: Participation
definition: I take part in tasks carried out by pupils, adopting a learner, model, and/or co-creator stance.

title: Team spirit
definition: I foster group work, collaboration, cooperation, and team spirit among pupils.

title: Flexibility
definition: I show flexibility to respond to pupils’ needs and draw on their reactions.

title: Right to fail
definition: I encourage risk-taking, letting go, and the right to make mistakes within the creative process.

title: Planning
definition: The educational team plans, in collaboration with partners, the integration of interventions within pupils’ PECA pathway, ensuring coherence and complementarity. PECA is valued in the school’s development plan.

title: PECA resource persons
definition: The educational team draws on resource persons for PECA (relay teachers, cultural coordinators, inspectors, school advisers, cultural operators, etc.).

title: Evaluation
definition: The educational team evaluates (with partners where relevant) the integration of interventions in pupils’ pathways, their diversity, and coordination, for improvement—for example by contrasting Ruches.

title: Showcasing
definition: The educational team regularly uses and animates ‘arts and culture’ spaces within the school (displaying pupils’ and artists’ works, information on academies, posters for local exhibitions or shows, quotes from artists, etc.).

title: Audiovisual
definition: Viewing, analysis, critique, scripting, production, editing...

title: Music
definition: Listening, choral singing, polyrhythm, sound creation, DIY instrument-making, orchestral practices...

title: French language and literature
definition: Reading, creative writing, slam, storytelling, narration...

title: Visual and plastic arts
definition: Painting, sculpture, photography, interactive installation, augmented reality, web art...

title: Theatre
definition: Diction, eloquence, acting, improvisation, community theatre, puppetry...

title: Fairground, circus and street arts
definition: Juggling, acrobatics, automaton manipulation, clowning, magic, street art...

title: Cultural heritage
definition: Historic sites and monuments, tales and legends, traditional songs and crafts...

title: Dance
definition: Choreography, interpretation, physical expression, mime...
